project: Declarative CI Step Tracking for Watcher
goal: Turn ci-watcher from repo-specific instrumentation into a config-driven, reusable CI observability toolkit for self-hosted GitHub Actions runners, while minimizing repeated watcher-specific edits in CI.yml.

summary.problem: The current workflow requires manual watcher wiring in CI.yml whenever new tracked jobs or step boundaries are added.
summary.approach: Introduce a generated CI inventory, one editable watcher manifest, and one generic runtime wrapper so tracking behavior is driven by config rather than repeated custom workflow code.
non_goals_for_v1: Do not infer shell subcommands inside arbitrary run blocks.
Do not redesign the live or final report yet.
Do not remove watcher startup and shutdown from CI.yml.

defaults.metrics_configuration: Inline per job/step in the same manifest

phases.objective: Create a deterministic, generated representation of the CI workflow structure so watcher-related tracking can reference jobs and step names without manually duplicating CI.yml content.

tasks.title: Parse CI.yml into a normalized inventory
tasks.description: Read .github/workflows/CI.yml and extract job ids, optional job display names, and ordered named step entries into a deterministic generated file.
notes: Ignore anonymous steps and do not parse shell subcommands in v1.
Preserve step order exactly as it appears in CI.yml.

tasks.title: Make generated output deterministic and reviewable
tasks.description: Ensure repeated runs on unchanged CI.yml produce stable output that is easy to diff in code review.
notes: Do not include noisy metadata that changes every run unless it is intentionally useful.

phases.objective: Introduce one editable config file that becomes the canonical source of truth for what should be tracked and which metrics should be collected.

tasks.title: Define manifest schema for jobs and steps
tasks.description: Create a compact YAML schema that models tracked jobs and their tracked named steps.
notes: Jobs default to true for tracking in v1.
Steps default to false for tracking in v1.

tasks.title: Keep metrics configuration inline in the manifest
tasks.description: Store toggles such as storage, docker, cpu, memory, and inode in the same manifest entry rather than splitting them into a second file.
notes: Use manifest-level defaults with per-entry overrides.

phases.objective: Prevent drift between the generated CI inventory and the editable watcher manifest so step tracking remains reliable as the workflow evolves.

tasks.title: Validate manifest job ids against generated inventory
tasks.description: Check that every job configured in the watcher manifest exists in the generated inventory and report any unknown or missing job ids.

tasks.title: Validate step names under each job
tasks.description: Ensure each manifest step name maps to a named step in the extracted workflow inventory for that job.
notes: Validation should be conservative and human-readable.

tasks.title: Keep validation outside the critical runtime path
tasks.description: Validation failures should be surfaced as a checker or explicit CI step rather than causing the watcher runtime to fail unexpectedly.

phases.objective: Replace scattered direct watcher boundary calls with one generic helper that decides at runtime whether a job or step should be tracked.

tasks.title: Create generic tracked-step wrapper
tasks.description: Build a reusable helper that receives job id and step name, consults the watcher manifest, and decides whether to emit begin/end capture boundaries.
notes: Reuse the existing mark_step.sh mechanism in v1.
Do not redesign watcher.sh in this phase.

tasks.title: Keep wrapper failures non-fatal
tasks.description: Ensure tracking decisions or metric collection failures do not break the underlying CI step execution.
outputs: logging for skipped or invalid entries

phases.objective: Reduce repeated watcher-specific logic in CI.yml while preserving explicit lifecycle control and compatibility with the current pipeline.

tasks.title: Keep explicit watcher lifecycle in CI.yml
tasks.description: Retain startup, shutdown, and final report generation as explicit workflow steps while limiting per-step tracking logic to the generic wrapper.

tasks.title: Migrate selected tracked steps to the generic wrapper
tasks.description: Replace direct watcher begin/end calls in representative jobs such as build, test, preview-up, and cleanup with the generic wrapper.
notes: Use a gradual migration rather than rewriting the full workflow at once.

tasks.title: Preserve coarse live stage updates for now
tasks.description: Retain /tmp/ci-stage-* writes for high-level progress until a future phase replaces or unifies that mechanism.

phases.objective: Make ci-watcher easier to adopt in other self-hosted GitHub Actions projects with minimal workflow-specific customization.

tasks.title: Define a minimal adoption contract
tasks.description: Document the smallest required integration surface so a new project can adopt ci-watcher with setup, finalize, and optional tracked-step wrapper usage.

tasks.title: Separate reusable conventions from repo-specific assumptions
tasks.description: Identify which parts of the current Xenium-specific flow must remain project-specific and which can become generic defaults.
notes: Examples include preview stack specifics, compose project naming, and repo-specific report sections.

scenarios: Extract inventory from the current CI.yml and confirm all expected major jobs are present.
Confirm ordered named steps are preserved for build, test, preview-up, and cleanup.
Validate a manifest that includes tracked jobs and tracked steps against the generated inventory.
Run the generic wrapper for one tracked step and one untracked step and verify begin/end behavior differs appropriately.
Confirm watcher runtime remains non-fatal when the manifest is missing or contains entries that do not match a runtime step.
Confirm current report generation and stats artifact flow remain intact.
acceptance_criteria: One generated inventory file exists and is deterministic.
One editable watcher manifest exists and can control job/step tracking.
A generic wrapper can replace repeated direct watcher step calls for selected jobs.
The current CI watcher and report pipeline still function without regressions.

assumptions: Named GitHub Actions steps are the only supported step granularity in v1.
Shell subcommand inference inside run blocks is out of scope for v1.
Watcher startup and final teardown remain explicit in CI.yml.
The generated inventory file is read-only and not the editable source of truth.
The editable watcher manifest is the canonical tracking configuration.
